title: Thrixspermum insularum
intro: Thrixspermum insularum is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Asia, commonly found in forests and grasslands. The plant is valued for its traditional medicinal properties, which include treating fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, making it a potential remedy for conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. Bioactive constituents of Thrixspermum insularum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Thrixspermum insularum is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are consumed as tonics to promote overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Thrixspermum insularum belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family consists of approximately 32,000 known species of orchids worldwide. The genus Thrixspermum is a group of terrestrial orchids that are primarily found in tropical regions. Thrixspermum insularum is a specific species within this genus. It is classified as a type of orchid native to the Philippines.
